concepts.lesson: Surface area is the total area of all the outer faces or surfaces of a solid object. It is measured in square units and depends on the shape and dimensions of the solid. Surface area calculations are commonly used in painting, covering, and material estimation problems.
concepts.real_example: Calculating the amount of paint needed to paint a water tank.

concepts.lesson: Volume is the amount of space occupied by a solid object and is measured in cubic units. It represents capacity and depends on the dimensions of the object. Volume calculations are essential in storage, packaging, and construction problems.
concepts.real_example: Finding the capacity of a water tank.

concepts.lesson: A cylinder is a solid with two equal circular bases connected by a curved surface. Its surface area and volume depend on the radius of the base and height. Cylinders are commonly seen in everyday objects.

A cone is a solid with a circular base and a curved surface that tapers to a single point called the vertex. Cone formulas involve radius, height, and slant height, and are important in geometry and real-life applications.

A sphere is a perfectly round solid where all points on the surface are equidistant from the centre. Unlike other solids, a sphere has no edges or vertices. Its symmetry makes its surface area and volume formulas unique and widely applicable.
